title: Roknifard: US-Iran Talks Only Substantial If Fights Stop
title_cn: 罗克尼法德：只有停止战斗，美伊才会进行实质性会谈
summary: US & Iran have agreed to cease attacking each other as peace talks resume. The exchange of attacks began over the Strait of Hormuz after the IRGC struck a container ship, prompting Washington to hit Iran, and both sides blaming the other for breaching the ceasefire. Julia Roknifard, Senior Lecturer at the School of Law & Governance at Taylor's University, Malaysia spoke to Bloomberg’s Jennifer Zabasajja on Horizons Middle East and Africa on the likely scenario ahead. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Crealights Technology on Breakeven Outlook

summary: Chinese optical module developer Crealights will break even as soon as this year due to strong demand. The company's CEO, Hu Zhaoyang, speaks to Minmin Low after its shares began trading in Hong Kong. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Pakistan Strikes Kill 29 Militants in New Afghan Border Flare-Up

summary: Pakistan said it carried out a ground operation and air strikes along its border with Afghanistan on Sunday and Monday, killing 29 militants in response to earlier attacks and marking the biggest flare-up in cross-border violence in weeks.

title: Hormuz Traffic Drops Off as Vessel Attacks Raise Fresh Concerns

summary: Kyle Rodda of Capital.com shares his views on the de-dollarization trade and market volatility. He says it shouldn't be understated "how significant it is that the United States is inviting Iran, effectively, back into global trade, selling its oil into global markets." (Source: Bloomberg)

title: China Says Tech Growth a Challenge to Predicting Energy Demand

title: Philippines Sees Slower Growth, Weak Peso Beyond Marcos’ Term
title_cn: 马科斯任期结束后菲律宾经济增长放缓、比索疲软
summary: The Philippines has cut its economic growth targets and sees a weaker peso beyond the end of the term of President Ferdinand Marcos Jr. in 2028 amid headwinds such as the Middle East tensions and an intense El Niño weather event.

title: Kent on Reserve Bank of Australia's New Framework
title_cn: 肯特谈澳大利亚储备银行的新框架
summary: Australian central bank Assistant Governor Chris Kent said the institution will be better prepared to respond to the next crisis it faces following a review of alternative monetary policy tools. In a speech in Sydney, Kent, who oversees financial markets at the Reserve Bank, said the cash rate target remains the primary and preferred instrument, but additional tools can play an important role during extraordinary times and provide some extra support. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: US and Iran Agree to Halt Attacks Ahead of Talks
title_cn: 美国和伊朗同意在会谈前停止袭击
summary: The US and Iran have agreed to stop attacking each other before peace talks resume this week over the Strait of Hormuz and other issues, paving the way to end days of tit-for-tat attacks that tested a fragile truce. Bloomberg's Wendy Benjaminson breaks down the latest developments. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: RBA Will Be Better Prepared to Handle Next Crisis, Kent Says

title: South Korea Unveils Plan to Sustain Lead in AI
title_cn: 韩国公布维持人工智能领先地位的计划
summary: South Korea unveiled an ambitious plan aimed at cementing its status as a technological powerhouse, with companies led by Samsung Electronics Co. and SK Hynix Inc. initiating large-scale investments in memory chips, data centers and robotics. Samsung and SK Hynix leaders sat next to President Lee Jae Myung at a briefing, and are expected to give more details of their future investment plans. Bloomberg's Cat Barton reports from Seoul. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Anthropic's Mythos 5 Cleared by US for Wider Use
title_cn: Anthropic 的 Mythos 5 已获美国批准以供更广泛使用
summary: Anthropic PBC won US approval to restore some access to its powerful Mythos 5 artificial intelligence model, after resolving concerns from Donald Trump's administration about the technology’s potential threats to national security. Bloomberg's Neil Campling reports. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Samsung, SK Prep Record Spending to Sustain Korea’s AI Lead

title: German AI Rollout Offers €300 Billion Fix for Worker Shortage
title_cn: 德国人工智能推出 3000 亿欧元解决工人短缺问题
summary: At a made-to-order homebuilder in northwest Germany, processing more than 250 invoices a week used to swallow the equivalent of four working days. After introducing artificial intelligence last year, the task takes half as long.

title: Australia to Strengthen Enforcement of Under-16 Social Media Ban
title_cn: 澳大利亚将加强对 16 岁以下社交媒体禁令的执行
summary: Australia will grant its online safety watchdog more powers and increase the maximum penalty for breaches of its world-first social media ban, with Prime Minister Anthony Albanese saying big tech companies are not doing enough to comply.

title: China's Commerce Minister Visits Europe for Trade Talks
title_cn: 中国商务部长访问欧洲进行贸易谈判
summary: EU Trade Commissioner Maros Sefcovic is set to meet Chinese Commerce Minister Wang Wentao in Brussels this week as the bloc presses Beijing to address a widening trade imbalance that reached about €360 billion ($410 billion) in 2025 and continued to expand this year. Bloomberg's Richard Bravo has more. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: UK's Burnham Set to Pledge Major Devolution in Speech
title_cn: 英国伯纳姆将承诺在言论上进行重大权力下放
summary: Andy Burnham will pledge the biggest devolution of power in England in modern times in his first major speech since confirming his intention to succeed Keir Starmer as prime minister. Bloomberg Opinion's Rosa Prince has a preview of the speech. Her views are her own. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: China Blacklists More Japan Firms as Takaichi Feud Deepens
title_cn: 随着高市不和加深，中国将更多日本公司列入黑名单
summary: China expanded its export-control offensive against Japan, a move that affects 40 more entities and signals a deepening feud with Prime Minister Sanae Takaichi’s government.

title: Airplane Hitting Beijing Tower Reveals Security Risk Near Xi
title_cn: 飞机撞上北京塔揭示习近平附近的安全风险
summary: A small plane that crashed into Beijing’s tallest skyscraper on Friday has exposed a vulnerability in the Chinese capital’s controlled airspace, prompting questions over safeguards around politically sensitive locations.

title: Japan Protests China’s Maritime Claims After Bomber Flights
title_cn: 日本在轰炸机飞行后抗议中国的海事主张
summary: Japan has protested activity by Chinese coast guard vessels asserting Beijing’s claims to maritime borders close to a southern Japanese island, Tokyo’s top government spokesman said, days after Tokyo scrambled fighter jets in response to flights by Chinese and Russian bombers around Japan’s south.

title: Man Group’s Ed Cole on AI, Inflation and the Changing Global Order
title_cn: 英仕曼集团的 Ed Cole 谈人工智能、通货膨胀和不断变化的全球秩序
summary: Despite agreeing that AI is likely as transformative as its proponents argue, Ed Cole believes it’s too early to understand whether there’s a bubble. Meanwhile, the growing backlash against globalization and an effort to reshore means traditional 60/40 portfolios are at risk of becoming outdated. A reallocation of assets across gold, small caps and liquid alternatives could be essential to keep up with a changing macro environment. Cole is the head of multi-strategy equities at Man Group, the world’s largest publicly-traded hedge fund. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Australia, Vanuatu Agree Deal After Impasse Over China Ties
title_cn: 澳大利亚与瓦努阿图在中国关系陷入僵局后达成协议
summary: Australia and the Pacific island nation of Vanuatu have sealed a cooperation deal, with the final agreement reported to be substantially revised from a preliminary accord signed last year.

title: Kennedy Center Lauds Bill Maher With Its Own Future Unknown
title_cn: 肯尼迪中心称赞比尔·马赫的未来未知
summary: The Kennedy Center on Sunday awarded Bill Maher the Mark Twain Prize for American Humor, lifting up a comedian who has tussled with President Donald Trump at a time when the arts institution’s future — and the control Trump has over it — has come into question.

title: Singapore’s Main Opposition Keeps Singh as Chief After Vote
title_cn: 新加坡主要反对党投票后维持辛格为首席
summary: Pritam Singh kept his role as leader of Singapore’s main opposition Workers’ Party after a special vote by cadres following his convictions for lying to a parliamentary committee.

title: US and Iran Agree to Halt Attacking Each Other Ahead of Talks
title_cn: 美国和伊朗同意在会谈前停止相互攻击
summary: The US and Iran have agreed to stop attacking each other before peace talks resume this week over the Strait of Hormuz and other issues, paving the way to end days of tit-for-tat attacks that tested a fragile truce.

title: Russia Ready to Continue Discussing Ukraine With US, Putin Says
title_cn: 普京称俄罗斯准备继续与美国讨论乌克兰问题
summary: The Kremlin expects US negotiators Steve Witkoff and Jared Kushner to travel to Moscow to continue talks on ending the war in Ukraine once Washington is no longer preoccupied with the conflict with Iran, Russian President Vladimir Putin said in an interview with state television.

title: UK Plans ‘Hybrid’ Warship to Replace Aging Destroyer Fleet
title_cn: 英国计划用“混合”军舰取代老化的驱逐舰舰队
summary: Britain has scrapped plans to replace its aging warships with a new destroyer and will instead procure at least six so-called common combat vessels as it seeks to prepare the military for modern warfare.

title: Australia’s Ruling Labor Party Retakes Poll Lead From Right-Wing One Nation
title_cn: 澳大利亚执政工党从右翼一国党手中夺回民调领先地位
summary: Australia’s ruling Labor Party has retaken the lead in opinion polls, pushing the insurgent right-wing One Nation party into second place.

title: Putin Acknowledges Russia Faces Fuel Supply Problems
title_cn: 普京承认俄罗斯面临燃料供应问题
summary: Russian President Vladimir Putin said fuel supply problems persist for motorists and businesses, including queues at gas stations, as Moscow weighs measures to stabilize the domestic market after refinery outages.

title: Venezuela’s Machado Attempts Fast Return Amid Quake Response
title_cn: 委内瑞拉马查多试图在地震响应中快速返回
summary: Venezuela’s opposition leader María Corina Machado is working to return to the country as soon as possible, according to people with direct knowledge of her plans, a move that could reshape the political landscape as acting President Delcy Rodríguez struggles to respond to the nation’s worst natural disaster in decades.

title: Trump’s U-Turn on Iran Sanctions Would Unravel Decades of Curbs
title_cn: 特朗普对伊朗制裁的大转变将瓦解数十年的限制
summary: The Trump administration’s effort to unwind decades of sanctions as part of a deal to end the war with Iran has created a head-spinning situation for governments, banks and other companies as they contemplate a shifting patchwork of new permissions and old restrictions.

title: Trump Shifts Messaging from Iran to Economy
title_cn: 特朗普将信息从伊朗转向经济
summary: Bloomberg News White House Correspondent Jeff Mason joins Bloomberg This Weekend and explains that President Donald Trump is increasingly shifting his public message from the conflict with Iran back to the economy, highlighting lower oil and gasoline prices as the White House seeks to refocus on voters' economic concerns ahead of the midterms. And yet, a recent report has come out that a missed intelligence update and disconnected Pentagon databases contributed to a deadly strike on a former Iranian military site that had been converted into an elementary school Bloomberg News Technology & National Security Reporter Katrina Manson explains to hosts David Gura and Christina Ruffini. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: NY Democratic Nominee Cait Conley: Voters Reject Dysfunction
title_cn: 纽约民主党候选人凯特·康利：选民拒绝功能障碍
summary:
Democratic Nominee for New York 17th House District Cait Conley joins Bloomberg This Weekend to discuss the upcoming general election against incumbent Republican Congressman Mike Lawler. 

 (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Supreme Court Leaves Trump’s Fed, Citizenship Gambits for Last
title_cn: 最高法院将特朗普的美联储和公民权策略留到最后
summary: President Donald Trump is on the brink of learning whether the US Supreme Court will bless two of his most audacious gambits, his bids to oust a Federal Reserve governor and roll back automatic birthright citizenship.

title: Mamdani Says Primaries Show Voters Want a New Kind of Politics
title_cn: 马姆达尼表示，初选表明选民想要一种新型政治
summary: New York City Mayor Zohran Mamdani said he believes Tuesday’s state primaries, where the slate of progressive candidates won, show that American voters are looking for a “new kind of politics.”

title: Ugandan Army Chief Shuts Down Main Independent Media Group
title_cn: 乌干达陆军参谋长关闭主要独立媒体集团
summary: Uganda’s army chief, the son of long-serving president Yoweri Museveni, ordered the country’s main independent media group to halt operations, after accusing it of biased reporting.

title: Strategist Sees WTI Falling to $40 a Barrel
title_cn: 策略师预计 WTI 跌至每桶 40 美元
summary: Rising oil prices are accelerating a supply glut in the Western Hemisphere, Bloomberg Intelligence Senior Commodity Strategist Mike McGlone argues that growing production and improving technology could drive WTI crude down to $40 a barrel. McGlone explains to David Gura and Christina Ruffini on Bloomberg This Weekend that gas prices are likely to be lower by the midterm elections, but warns that such declines have often coincided with weakness in the stock market. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Ukraine Targets Russian Refineries in Fresh Drone Strikes
title_cn: 乌克兰新一轮无人机袭击瞄准俄罗斯炼油厂
summary: Ukraine struck the Slavyansk-na-Kubani oil refinery in Russia’s Krasnodar region and “reached” a refinery in the Yaroslavl region overnight, President Volodymyr Zelenskyy said on X, calling the attacks part of Kyiv’s “long-range sanctions” campaign against Moscow.

title: S. Korea to Unveil ‘Three Mega Projects’ With Samsung, SK Group
title_cn: 韩国将与三星、SK 集团推出“三大项目”
summary: South Korea will unveil a sweeping strategy on Monday aimed at driving the nation’s great leap forward, with Samsung Electronics and SK Group set to announce major investment plans alongside new government policy initiatives.
